content:
# Phosphorite Deposits

**Summary**: Sedimentary rocks containing significant amounts of phosphate minerals.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[1996-jarad|Jarad, 1996]] studied the phosphorite deposits in the Sirhan-Turayf Basin.

- [[1996-matter|Matter, 1996]] investigated the mineralogical and geochemical characteristics of phosphorite deposits.

## Scientific Context

- [[1996-jarad|Jarad, 1996]] The primary economic mineral target in the study area.

- [[1996-matter|Matter, 1996]] investigated the mineralogical and geochemical characteristics of phosphorite deposits.

## Measurement and Characterization Methods

- [[1996-jarad|Jarad, 1996]] Chemical analysis of drill-hole samples.

## Key Findings Across Sources

- The Hazm al-Jalamid deposit is noted for its exceptionally high grade and thickness, occurring in two distinct horizons (Horizon 1 and Horizon 3) separated by phosphatic limestone (Horizon 2). ([[1996-jarad|Jarad, 1996, p. 170]])

- The ore is primarily composed of microcrystalline apatite, dolomite, and calcite. ([[1996-matter|Matter, 1996, p. 26]])
